title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S13
body:
**Scene 13: 16:00–17:30 · The Bible on the Table**

HERO FRAME: Close on the open Bible on the library table · the page open to Revelation 3:20 · the verse subtly highlighted (a faint underline) — *"Behold, I stand at the door, and knock: if any man hear my voice, and open the door, I will come in to him, and will sup with him, and he with me."*

MOTION: kling — slow camera push toward the open page. The highlighted verse glows almost imperceptibly. A single dust mote drifts past in the lamplight.

MOOD: Setup for the closing payoff — the audience sees this verse before Walter recognizes it.

AUDIO: Walter: *"I have been reading the Bible. I am not a man of faith. I am a man of numbers. But the Bible is the only book I have found that talks about being the last man, or the first one. The other books all assume there are many."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
